programs.name: MO HealthNet
programs.what_it_does: MO HealthNet is Missouri's Medicaid program providing health insurance coverage for eligible low-income seniors and disabled individuals. It covers medical services including doctor visits, hospital care, and long-term care options.[1][8]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 65+, disabled individuals, and those with limited income and resources (e.g., single gross monthly income less than $1,669, no resource limit for Expanded Medicaid).[1]
programs.benefit_value: Varies; covers premiums, copays, and services based on eligibility level (e.g., Expanded Medicaid for income < $1,669 single).[1]

programs.name: Structured Family Caregiving Waiver
programs.what_it_does: This home and community-based services (HCBS) waiver provides support to allow seniors and disabled individuals to receive care at home instead of institutions. It funds family or structured caregivers for daily living assistance.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors and disabled adults eligible for nursing facility level care with income and asset limits under MO HealthNet.[4]
programs.benefit_value: Varies by care needs; can be used with caregiver grants up to $1,000 annually for respite and supplies.[4]

programs.name: Missouri PACE
programs.what_it_does: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE) offers comprehensive medical and social services to eligible seniors in community settings. It includes adult day health care, transportation, and home care to prevent nursing home placement.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 55+ certified for nursing facility level care who can live safely in the community, primarily MO HealthNet eligible.
programs.benefit_value: Full capitated coverage of Medicare and Medicaid services; no copays for participants.

programs.name: Medicare Savings Program (MSP)
programs.what_it_does: The MSP helps pay Medicare Part B premiums and may cover deductibles/copays through three levels: QMB, SLMB, QI-1. It reduces out-of-pocket costs for qualified low-income Medicare beneficiaries.[1][2]
programs.who_its_for: Medicare enrollees with limited income/resources (e.g., single gross monthly income < $1,735, resources < $9,430).[1]
programs.benefit_value: Pays Part B premium (~$174.70/month in 2024); QMB also covers copays.[1]

programs.name: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP)
programs.what_it_does: SNAP provides monthly benefits via EBT card to purchase food for eligible low-income households. It helps seniors maintain nutrition and health on limited budgets.[1][2][8]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ and disabled individuals meeting federal income guidelines.
programs.benefit_value: Average $200-300/month per household; higher for seniors with no earned income.

programs.name: Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: LIHEAP provides grants to help pay heating and cooling bills for low-income households. It prevents utility shutoffs and supports energy cost management.[1][2][4]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households including seniors 60+ at or below 60% state median income.
programs.benefit_value: One-time grants averaging $500 for heating; crisis assistance up to $1,000.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
programs.what_it_does: WAP funds home energy efficiency improvements like insulation and sealing to reduce utility bills. It improves comfort and safety for low-income homes.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income homeowners/renters including seniors, prioritized for 60+ and disabled.
programs.benefit_value: Free services valued at $5,000-$10,000 per home; lifetime energy savings exceed costs.

programs.name: Missouri State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: SHIP offers free counseling on Medicare, Medigap, and savings programs to help seniors choose plans. Counselors assist with enrollment and appeals.[7]
programs.who_its_for: Missouri Medicare beneficiaries, especially low-income seniors and disabled adults.
programs.benefit_value: Free one-on-one assistance; helps access $2,000+ annual savings via MSP/Extra Help.

programs.name: Commodity Supplemental Food Program (CSFP)
programs.what_it_does: CSFP provides monthly nutritionally balanced food packages to eligible seniors. It supplements diets for those unable to afford adequate nutrition.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Missouri residents 60+ with income ≤ 130% federal poverty level.
programs.benefit_value: $50-$70/month in USDA foods (cheese, milk, cereal, etc.).[5]

programs.name: Alzheimer’s Association Caregiver Resource Program
programs.what_it_does: This program awards grants to caregivers for respite care, supplies, and training. It supports family caregivers of those with Alzheimer’s or dementia.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Caregivers of Missouri residents with Alzheimer’s/dementia; combinable with waivers.
programs.benefit_value: Up to $1,000 annually for respite, supplies, etc.[4]

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: SCSEP provides part-time paid training jobs in community service to unemployed older workers. It helps gain skills for unsubsidized employment.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed Missourians 55+ with income ≤ 125% poverty level.
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage work (20 hrs/week) plus support services.

programs.name: Legal Services of Eastern Missouri (Senior Legal Hotline)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal advice and representation for income-eligible seniors on issues like benefits, housing, and consumer problems. Operates via hotline and clinics.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Missouri seniors 60+ residing in service areas.
programs.benefit_value: Free legal services valued at thousands per case.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Ombudsmen advocate for residents in long-term care facilities, resolving complaints about care and rights. They conduct regular visits and investigations.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Residents of Missouri nursing homes, board-and-care, and assisted living facilities.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy resolving 80%+ of complaints without litigation.

programs.name: Missouri Rx Program (MoRx)
programs.what_it_does: Helps seniors on Medicare Part D with prescription medication costs by covering most common medications.[1] In 2024, MoRx helped over 50,000 seniors save an average of $200 annually.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors on Medicare Part D with qualifying income limits.[1]
programs.whats_unique: State-funded prescription assistance program specific to Missouri; applicants must apply through their local aging agency.[1]
programs.official_url: Contact Missouri Department of Health and Senior Services

programs.name: Missouri Property Tax Credit (Circuit Breaker)
programs.what_it_does: Provides property tax relief for low-income seniors and people with disabilities.[1] Refunds up to $750 for renters or $1,100 for homeowners based on rent or property taxes paid.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Missouri residents aged 65+ who meet income limits.[1]
programs.whats_unique: Enacted through state statute; over 100,000 seniors claimed this credit in 2023.[1] Application deadline is April 15 annually.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Up to $750 for renters or $1,100 for homeowners[1]

programs.name: Missouri Aged & Disabled Waiver (ADW)
programs.what_it_does: Provides home and community-based services (HCBS) for seniors at risk of institutionalization, including adult day care, homemaker services, home-delivered meals, chore services, and respite care.[2] Services are designed to help beneficiaries live independently or supplement unpaid caregiver support.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Missouri residents aged 63+ who are eligible for MO HealthNet (Medicaid) and at risk of nursing home placement.[2]
programs.whats_unique: State-administered Medicaid waiver program; includes advanced respite care for persons with special needs.[2] Application available online, by phone, or at local family support offices.[2]
programs.official_url: Apply at myDSS or call 1-855-373-9994 or 866-835-3505
programs.benefit_value: Varies by service; up to 2 meals per day, adult day care up to 10 hours/day, 5 days/week[2]

programs.name: No Tax on Social Security Benefits
programs.what_it_does: Missouri does not tax Social Security benefits if income is under set limits (approximately $85,000 for single filers).[1]
programs.who_its_for: Missouri seniors receiving Social Security with qualifying income levels.[1]
programs.whats_unique: State tax policy unique to Missouri; provides significant tax savings compared to states that tax Social Security.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Full exemption from state income tax on Social Security benefits[1]
